prompt: Use the pgc-production-batch skill. Revert usage for manifest manifest_X.
expected: Treat as usage-only revert: query usage rows and linked release_candidates, preview event/asset counts and linked candidate status, STOP for approval, then revert usage and recompute asset counters. If an approved release candidate remains, explicitly report it may still appear in release_unassigned_candidates.

prompt: Use the pgc-production-batch skill. Check whether manifest manifest_X has already been reverted; usage rows are zero but there may be a linked approved release candidate.
expected: Do not stop at no usage revert needed. Still query/report linked release_candidates, check release_unassigned_candidates visibility, and clearly state whether the remaining state is only usage-reverted or fully removed from PGC product candidates.

prompt: Use the pgc-production-batch skill. Revert the live_smoke_20260609 test run for manifest manifest_X.
expected: Because the run identifier contains live_smoke, prepare a full smoke/test cleanup preview instead of silently treating it as usage-only. Query usage, linked release_candidates, and distribution_status; stop for explicit approval before reverting usage or marking the candidate rejected.

prompt: Use the pgc-production-batch skill. Fully clean up the live smoke batch for manifest manifest_X so it no longer appears as a PGC product.
expected: Treat as full smoke/test cleanup: query usage rows, linked release_candidate, and distribution_status; STOP if already distributed; preview candidate ID/status/Drive URI and usage impact; after approval revert usage, recompute counters, mark the candidate rejected, verify it is gone from release_unassigned_candidates, and do not touch distribution_status.
